sql_array: CREATE TABLE employees (id INT, name VARCHAR, department VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO employees VALUES (1, 'John Doe', 'Engineering'), (2, 'Jane Smith', 'Marketing'), (3, 'Sara Lee', 'Engineering');
SELECT name, department, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY id) AS row_num FROM employees;
UPDATE employees SET department = 'Human Resources' WHERE id = 2;
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS department_count, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY department) AS rank FROM employees GROUP BY department;
